senators.name: Martin Heinrich
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in mechanical engineering from University of Missouri. Worked as a mechanical engineer before entering politics. One of very few members of the Senate with an engineering degree. Serves on the Senate Armed Services and Energy committees where he applies technical expertise to defense technology, nuclear weapons modernization, and clean energy policy. Engineering background provides genuine analytical and problem-solving foundation.
education: B.S. Mechanical Engineering, University of Missouri

senators.name: Ben Ray Lujan
senators.notNerdReason: B.B.A. from New Mexico Highlands University. Career in IT at the New Mexico Cultural Affairs department before entering politics. Some technical background in information technology. Served as assistant House Democratic Whip and led Democrats' tech-forward campaign strategy. IT experience shows some data fluency but overall career is political rather than analytical.
experience: IT Professional, NM Cultural Affairs Department

NM-01.name: Melanie Stansbury
NM-01.nerdReason: B.A. from Sewanee: The University of the South, M.S. in natural resources from Cornell University, and M.P.P. from University of New Mexico. Multiple graduate degrees including a Cornell science degree. Career as water resources and environmental policy researcher at Sandia National Laboratories and the U.S. Senate. Applies scientific research expertise directly to natural resources and climate policy.
education: B.A., Sewanee: The University of the South
experience: Environmental Policy Researcher, Sandia National Laboratories

NM-02.name: Gabe Vasquez
NM-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from New Mexico State University. Career in community organizing, conservation, and local government (Las Cruces City Council). Community engagement background without analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NM-03.name: Teresa Leger Fernandez
NM-03.nerdReason: B.A. from Yale University and J.D. from Stanford Law School. Career as attorney specializing in affordable housing, community development, and Native American water rights. Yale + Stanford Law represents elite analytical training. Deep expertise in water law and community economic development built on rigorous legal research.
experience: Attorney, affordable housing and community development
